call it pre-product design.

had a pretty large design org over there, and Grammarly, there were like five, and hired a whole bunch of them.

I quickly realized I'm not going to make any money to survive, so I switched to computer science.
I started off in a marketing firm, a marketing startup, and yeah, that was great.
But then I started getting some freelancing work, and I was just doing too much.

I thought that I would just stay in development.
So I started like getting into more like products, like basically the whole app, doing it myself.

Oh, okay.
I thought you were frozen.
Sorry.
I'm listening.
I'm listening.

Like there's so many ways you could go about it.

Um, and I was getting more into like performance.
you about

think performance might be more important.

changes into their code base.

Pretty cool.

So, yeah.

Sorry?

are If anyone is, don't child If

out don't have Absolutely.

let's a look

Center, post, post, Hi線 Adios.

or have been onboarding or just onboarded the past two months or so.

Thank Thank

So we're still building that software, so it's very much in alpha, and we're testing everything.

And hiring...

Generally, yes.

I can also do front end.
But when it comes to owning the, I would say, the design cycles, the design process, yeah, that's something that you'd own and push forward autonomously.
I think that's all my questions.
Awesome, awesome, awesome.
